response.justification: Aisha brings genuine high-throughput distributed systems experience from Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP requests per second, low-latency async I/O with DLang), current hands-on exposure to agentic workflows and MCP tooling at re:cinq, and a consistent Staff-level IC track record. However, Python — a stated must-have — is entirely absent from the resume and skills list, which is a material gap. Stakeholder management, operational on-call ownership, and agile ceremony experience are also underrepresented, and the AI/agentic work at the current role is too recent and light on detail to confirm depth.
strengths: Demonstrated high-throughput, low-latency distributed systems experience handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby using async I/O, caching, and fibers
Active contribution to AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators at re:cinq, directly aligned with the AI-native engineering environment the role requires
Long track record of Staff-level IC work across diverse stacks and domains, showing breadth and adaptability
concerns: Python is completely absent from the resume and skills list despite being an explicit must-have; TypeScript is present but Python is the primary language requirement
No evidence of stakeholder management, cross-functional coordination, or bridging engineering and product perspectives at scale
AI and agentic experience at re:cinq started April 2025 and is described at a high level only, making depth difficult to assess
